content_en:
The definition of a Companion — a Sahabi — carries enormous weight in Islamic jurisprudence and hadith science. The most widely accepted definition, favored by Imam al-Bukhari and the majority of hadith scholars, is that a Companion is any Muslim who met the Prophet, upon him be peace, while believing in him, and who died as a Muslim. This definition is intentionally broad. It does not require that the person narrated any hadiths, fought in any battle, or spent years in the Prophet's company. A Bedouin who entered Medina, accepted Islam in the Prophet's presence, and then returned to his tribe — never meeting the Prophet again — is still considered a Companion. A child who was brought before the Prophet and received his supplication is recorded as a Companion. The breadth of the category reflects a theological reality: the honor of companionship is not proportional to the length or depth of association, but to the fact of believing encounter.

Ibn al-Athir organized Usd al-Ghabah alphabetically by the Arabic letter of each Companion's name, following the convention established by his predecessors. Within each letter, he arranged entries by the first name, then by the father's name, working through the kunya (the honorific Abu or Umm designation), and then the nisba (the tribal or geographic affiliation). This system allowed scholars to locate any named Companion quickly, and to cross-reference entries when a single individual was recorded under different names or designations in different sources.

The total number of Companions recorded in Usd al-Ghabah runs to approximately 7,500 entries. This figure reflects both the scale of the early Muslim community and the scope of Ibn al-Athir's research. Scholars estimate that the number of Companions who actually met the Prophet exceeded 100,000, particularly if one counts all those present at the Farewell Pilgrimage. However, only a fraction of these were named in the hadith literature and biographical tradition with enough information to merit a dedicated entry. Ibn al-Athir worked from the hadith collections, the musannaf works, and the earlier biographical dictionaries to compile as complete a list as the sources permitted.

In his methodology, Ibn al-Athir explicitly acknowledged his debt to two earlier major works: Ibn Abd al-Barr's Al-Isti'ab fi Ma'rifat al-Ashab and Ibn Manda's Al-Ma'rifah fi al-Sahabah. He used both as foundational sources, correcting errors he identified, reconciling conflicting reports, and adding entries that either scholar had omitted. He also cited the Tabaqat of Ibn Sa'd, the biographical sections of the major hadith collections, and regional biographical dictionaries for individual provinces of the early Islamic world.

One methodological feature that distinguishes Usd al-Ghabah is its care with disputed cases. Not every person recorded in earlier works was certainly a Companion — some entries reflected errors in transmission, confusion of names, or overly generous inclusion criteria. Ibn al-Athir flagged these cases, noting when scholars disagreed about whether a given individual truly met the Prophet or whether the account of their companionship was reliably transmitted. This critical dimension makes the work not merely a catalogue but a work of hadith scholarship in its own right.
content_id:
Definisi seorang Sahabat — seorang Shahabi — membawa bobot yang sangat besar dalam fikih Islam dan ilmu hadis. Definisi yang paling banyak diterima, yang difavoritkan oleh Imam al-Bukhari dan mayoritas ulama hadis, adalah bahwa seorang Sahabat adalah setiap Muslim yang bertemu Nabi ﷺ saat beriman kepadanya dan wafat sebagai Muslim. Definisi ini sengaja dibuat luas. Tidak mensyaratkan bahwa seseorang pernah meriwayatkan hadis, bertempur dalam peperangan apa pun, atau menghabiskan bertahun-tahun dalam kebersamaan dengan Nabi. Seorang Badui yang masuk Madinah, menerima Islam di hadapan Nabi, kemudian kembali ke sukunya — tidak pernah bertemu Nabi lagi — tetap dianggap seorang Sahabat. Seorang anak yang dihadirkan di hadapan Nabi dan menerima doanya tercatat sebagai Sahabat. Keluasan kategori ini mencerminkan realitas teologis: kehormatan persahabatan tidak proporsional dengan panjang atau kedalaman pergaulan, melainkan dengan fakta pertemuan yang beriman.

Ibn al-Athir mengorganisir Usd al-Ghabah secara alfabetis berdasarkan huruf Arab dari nama setiap Sahabat, mengikuti konvensi yang ditetapkan oleh para pendahulunya. Dalam setiap huruf, beliau menyusun entri-entri berdasarkan nama pertama, kemudian nama ayah, melalui kunya (sebutan kehormatan Abu atau Umm), lalu nisba (afiliasi suku atau geografis). Sistem ini memungkinkan para ulama menemukan Sahabat mana pun yang bernama dengan cepat, dan merujuk silang entri-entri ketika seorang individu dicatat dengan nama atau sebutan berbeda dalam sumber-sumber yang berbeda.

Jumlah total Sahabat yang tercatat dalam Usd al-Ghabah mencapai sekitar 7.500 entri. Angka ini mencerminkan baik skala komunitas Muslim awal maupun cakupan penelitian Ibn al-Athir. Para ulama memperkirakan bahwa jumlah Sahabat yang benar-benar bertemu Nabi melebihi 100.000, terutama jika menghitung semua yang hadir dalam Haji Wada'. Namun, hanya sebagian kecil dari mereka yang disebutkan namanya dalam literatur hadis dan tradisi biografi dengan informasi yang cukup untuk memerluakan entri khusus. Ibn al-Athir bekerja dari koleksi-koleksi hadis, karya-karya musannaf, dan kamus-kamus biografi terdahulu untuk menyusun daftar selengkap yang diizinkan oleh sumber-sumber yang ada.

Dalam metodologinya, Ibn al-Athir secara eksplisit mengakui hutangnya kepada dua karya besar terdahulu: Al-Isti'ab fi Ma'rifat al-Ashab karya Ibn Abd al-Barr dan Al-Ma'rifah fi al-Sahabah karya Ibn Mandah. Beliau menggunakan keduanya sebagai sumber-sumber dasar, mengoreksi kesalahan yang diidentifikasinya, mendamaikan laporan-laporan yang bertentangan, dan menambahkan entri-entri yang diabaikan oleh kedua ulama tersebut. Beliau juga mengutip Thabaqat Ibn Sa'd, bagian-bagian biografi dari koleksi-koleksi hadis utama, dan kamus-kamus biografi regional untuk provinsi-provinsi tertentu dari dunia Islam awal.

Salah satu ciri metodologis yang membedakan Usd al-Ghabah adalah kehati-hatiannya terhadap kasus-kasus yang diperdebatkan. Tidak setiap orang yang tercatat dalam karya-karya terdahulu sudah pasti seorang Sahabat — beberapa entri mencerminkan kesalahan dalam transmisi, kebingungan nama, atau kriteria penyertaan yang terlalu longgar. Ibn al-Athir menandai kasus-kasus ini, mencatat ketika para ulama tidak sepakat tentang apakah individu tertentu benar-benar bertemu Nabi atau apakah kisah persahabatan mereka ditransmisikan secara andal. Dimensi kritis ini menjadikan karya ini bukan sekadar katalog melainkan juga sebuah karya keilmuan hadis tersendiri.
content_ar: The definition of a Companion — a Sahabi — carries enormous weight in Islamic jurisprudence and الحديث science. The most widely accepted definition, favored by الإمام البخاري and the majority of الحديث العلماء, is that a Companion is any مسلم who met النبي ﷺ, upon him be peace, بينما believing in him, and who died as a مسلم. This definition is intentionally broad. It does not require that the person narrated any hadiths, fought in any battle, or spent years in النبي ﷺ's company. A Bedouin who entered المدينة المنورة, accepted الإسلام in النبي ﷺ's presence, and then returned to his tribe — never meeting النبي ﷺ again — is still considered a Companion. A الولد who was brought قبل النبي ﷺ and received his الدعاء is recorded as a Companion. The breadth of the category reflects a theological reality: the الشرف of companionship is not proportional to the length or depth of association, but to the fact of believing encounter. Ibn al-Athir organized Usd al-Ghabah alphabetically by the Arabic letter of each Companion's name, following the convention established by his predecessors. Within each letter, he arranged entries by the أولاً name, then by the الأب's name, working through the kunya (the honorific Abu or Umm designation), and then the nisba (the tribal or geographic affiliation). This system allowed العلماء to locate any named Companion quickly, and to cross-reference entries عندما a single individual was recorded under different names or designations in different sources. The total number of الصحابة recorded in Usd al-Ghabah runs to approximately 7,500 entries. This figure reflects both the scale of the early مسلم الجماعة and the scope of Ibn al-Athir's research. العلماء estimate that the number of الصحابة who actually met النبي ﷺ exceeded 100,000, particularly إذا one counts all those present at the Farewell Pilgrimage. غير أن, only a fraction of these were named in the الحديث literature and biographical التراث with enough information to merit a dedicated entry. Ibn al-Athir worked from the الحديث collections, the musannaf works, and the earlier biographical dictionaries to compile as complete a list as the sources permitted. In his methodology, Ibn al-Athir explicitly acknowledged his debt to two earlier major works: Ibn Abd al-Barr's Al-Isti'ab fi Ma'rifat al-Ashab and Ibn Manda's Al-Ma'rifah fi al-الصحابة. He used both as foundational sources, correcting errors he identified, reconciling conflicting reports, and adding entries that either scholar had omitted. He أيضاً cited the Tabaqat of Ibn Sa'd, the biographical sections of the major الحديث collections, and regional biographical dictionaries for individual provinces of the early Islamic الدنيا. One methodological feature that distinguishes Usd al-Ghabah is its care with disputed cases. Not every person recorded in earlier works was certainly a Companion — some entries reflected errors in transmission, confusion of name
